name: bury-my-heart-at-wounded-knee
description: Dee Brown's landmark history of the American West from the Native American perspective — understanding systemic injustice, the broken treaty cycle, heroic resistance against overwhelming odds, and cultural survival through eloquent testimony.
useCase: Recognizing patterns of systemic betrayal, deciding when to resist or surrender, preserving culture under threat, speaking truth to power through documentation, understanding how institutions break promises.
knownRisks: The book is deeply emotional and one-sided by design — it is not a neutral history
Some may find the relentless pattern of betrayal and violence overwhelming
Modern contexts differ from 19th-century colonialism — parallels require careful translation
